question: How does gin route requests through its middleware chain?

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command 'rg -n "type Context struct|type Engine struct|type RouterGroup struct" .'

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"func \\(engine \\*Engine\\) ServeHTTP|func \\(engine \\*Engine\\) handleHTTPRequest|func \\(c \\*Context\\) Next|func \\(c \\*Context\\) Abort|func \\(c \\*Context\\) IsAborted|combineHandlers|func \\(group \\*RouterGroup\\) Use|func \\(engine \\*Engine\\) HandleContext\" ."
graph.answer:
Gin builds and runs middleware as a single handler chain per route, not as a separate runtime stack.

- Middleware is assembled when routes are registered. `RouterGroup.Use` appends group middleware, `combineHandlers` concatenates group middleware with route handlers, and `addRoute` stores that final `HandlersChain` in the method tree. See [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L65), [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L241), and [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L364).
- A request enters `Engine.ServeHTTP`, which resets a pooled `Context` and sets `index = -1` and `handlers = nil` before dispatching to `handleHTTPRequest`. See [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L662) and [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L103).
- `handleHTTPRequest` selects the route tree for the HTTP method, matches the path, assigns the matched chain to `c.handlers`, sets `c.fullPath`, and then starts the chain with `c.Next()`. See [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L690).
- `Context.Next()` is the executor: it increments `c.index` and walks the `handlers` slice in order. That is why middleware can run code before `Next()` and then resume after downstream handlers finish. See [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L198).
- `Context.Abort()` stops traversal by setting `index` to `abortIndex`, so later `Next()` iterations stop skipping the rest of the chain. `IsAborted()` checks that sentinel. See [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L208).
- If no route matches, Gin replaces the chain with `allNoRoute` or `allNoMethod` and runs `serveError`, which itself calls `c.Next()` so those fallback handlers still execute. See [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L357), [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L738), and [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L764).

In short: route matching picks the chain, `Next()` walks it, and `Abort()` truncates it.
